seo.title: Building Voice Agents with OpenAI Agents SDK + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build voice agents with OpenAI Agents SDK + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: Building voice agents with OpenAI's Agents SDK enables real-time conversational AI through speech-to-text, multi-agent reasoning, and text-to-speech pipelines. Guardrails are essential due to sub-second latency demands and high-risk interactions where prompt injection, handoff loops, or unsafe responses can cause catastrophic failures. Vex provides output validation to ensure response appropriateness, safety, and coherence without compromising performance.

architectureOverview.heading: Voice Agents Architecture with OpenAI Agents SDK
architectureOverview.description:
Voice agents process audio streams through a low-latency pipeline: real-time STT (e.g., Whisper) transcribes speech, feeding into an OpenAI Agent that orchestrates specialized sub-agents for reasoning, fact-checking, and response generation. Agents handoff via the SDK's structured protocol, with guardrails at each step to validate outputs before TTS synthesis using services like ElevenLabs or OpenAI TTS.

The multi-agent system handles conversation state across turns, maintaining context while enforcing latency budgets (<500ms end-to-end). Tracing spans the entire pipeline to debug handoff failures or injection attempts.

Vex integrates as output parsers on each agent, verifying responses against safety, coherence, and performance SLAs before forwarding to TTS.

risks.name: Agent handoff loops between specialized agents
risks.description: Fact-check and response agents ping-pong indefinitely due to ambiguous handoff instructions
risks.mitigation: Vex validates handoff payloads for loop detection and enforces max iterations

risks.name: Guardrail bypasses via prompt injection
risks.description: Users inject instructions like 'ignore safety rules' that propagate through agent handoffs
risks.mitigation: Vex semantic analysis strips injections and validates response intent

risks.name: Tool output not validated before agent consumption
risks.description: Unstructured tool responses (e.g., malformed STT output) cause agent reasoning failures
risks.mitigation: Vex parses and normalizes tool outputs before agent input

risks.name: Tracing gaps in multi-agent handoff chains
risks.description: Lost context between STT вҶ’ agent вҶ’ TTS makes debugging latency violations impossible
risks.mitigation: Vex provides end-to-end tracing with latency budgets per pipeline stage

implementation.title: Basic Voice Agent Pipeline Setup
implementation.description: Initialize OpenAI Agents SDK with real-time STT and TTS integration
implementation.code:
from agents import Agent, Runner
from openai import OpenAI

# Voice pipeline agents
stt_agent = Agent(
    name="STT Agent",
    instructions="Transcribe audio accurately and pass clean text",
    model="whisper-1"
)

reasoning_agent = Agent(
    name="Reasoning Agent",
    instructions="Maintain conversation context, handoff when needed",
    model="gpt-4o"
)

runner = Runner([stt_agent, reasoning_agent])

# Real-time audio processing
def process_audio(audio_bytes):
    result = runner.run(audio_bytes, stream=True)
    return result.final_output

implementation.title: Multi-Agent Handoff with Conversation Memory
implementation.description: Implement handoff protocol with state persistence for coherent conversations

implementation.title: Latency-Monitored TTS Output
implementation.description: Enforce sub-second latency with performance tracing
implementation.code:
import time
from elevenlabs import generate, stream

def tts_with_latency_guard(text: str, max_latency=0.5):
    start_time = time.time()
    audio = generate(text=text, voice="default")
    latency = time.time() - start_time
    
    if latency > max_latency:
        raise TimeoutError(f"TTS exceeded {max_latency}s budget")
    
    return stream(audio)

# Integrate with agent output
agent_output = runner.run(input_text)
voice_response = tts_with_latency_guard(agent_output)

implementation.title: Real-Time STT Streaming
implementation.description: Process continuous audio streams with agent handoff triggers
implementation.code:
from openai import AsyncOpenAI
client = AsyncOpenAI()

async def stream_stt(audio_stream):
    transcript = ''
    async for chunk in client.audio.transcriptions.create(
        model="whisper-1",
        file=audio_stream,
        response_format="json",
        stream=True
    ):
        transcript += chunk.text
        if len(transcript) > 50:  # Handoff trigger
            yield handoff(reasoning_agent, transcript)
            transcript = ''

vexIntegration.title: Vex Output Guardrail on Primary Agent
vexIntegration.description: Add Vex as final output validator before TTS with safety + latency checks

vexIntegration.title: Handoff Loop Detection with Vex
vexIntegration.description: Prevent infinite agent handoffs using Vex state tracking
vexIntegration.code:
handoff_guard = Guardrail(
    name="Handoff Loop Detector",
    verifications=[
        "handoff_loop_depth<5",
        "handoff_target_valid"
    ]
)

def safe_handoff(from_agent, to_agent, payload):
    result = handoff(to_agent, payload)
    
    loop_check = verify(
        guardrail=handoff_guard,
        input=conversation_state,
        output=result
    )
    
    if loop_check.failed:
        return "Transfer failed - try rephrasing"
    
    conversation_state['handoffs'] += 1
    return loop_check.output

vexIntegration.title: Prompt Injection Defense Layer
vexIntegration.description: Semantic analysis blocks jailbreak attempts across agent chain
vexIntegration.code:
injection_guard = Guardrail(
    name="Prompt Injection Defense",
    verifications=[
        "no_prompt_injection",
        "safety_intent_alignment",
        "no_jailbreak_patterns"
    ]
)

# Wrap all agent inputs
async def protected_agent_run(agent, user_input):
    clean_input = verify(
        guardrail=injection_guard,
        input=user_input,
        context=conversation_state
    )
    
    if clean_input.failed:
        return "Invalid request"
    
    return await agent.run(clean_input.output)

# Usage
safe_response = protected_agent_run(reasoning_agent, stt_output)

cta.description: Add Vex guardrails today to protect your production voice agents from handoff failures and safety violations.
